---
title: "G.6 — RADIUS / 802.1X Modernization"
subtitle: "NPS-on-AD to ISE / Entra-RADIUS in the Hybrid-Cloud model"
doc-type: subcategory-leaf
pillar: modernization
sub-category: G
leaf: G.6
status: scaffold
date: 2026-05-07
aspirational: true
canon-source: "src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/adapters/radius/radius-adapter-interface.md"
repo-path: docs/customer-documents/modernization/network-transformation/radius-802-1x-modernization.qmd
---

# G.6 — RADIUS / 802.1X Modernization {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-warning}
## Scaffold — canonical adapter declared, customer-facing guide pending

The RADIUS / NPS adapter interface is canonical at
[`DM_030`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/adapters/radius/radius-adapter-interface.md).
This page is a stub that surfaces DM_030 in the customer-documents
taxonomy; the full operational guide (parallel to `dns-modernization.qmd`)
is pending.
:::

## Why this matters

Windows Network Policy Server (NPS) authenticates 802.1X wired/wireless,
VPN, and dial-up connections against Active Directory groups. Every
access point, VPN concentrator, and managed switch that uses RADIUS is
an AD dependency. If NPS goes down before the replacement RADIUS is
validated, users cannot connect to the network — this is not a degraded
experience, it is a total access failure.

DM_030 is rated **CRITICAL** in the directory-migration adapter priority
matrix for that reason; see
[Directory Migration Adapters — RADIUS / Network Policy Server](../../../modernization/adapters.qmd#radius)
for the full risk profile and migration sequence.

## Registered implementations

| Implementation        | Role                                  | Source                |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| `nps`                 | Migration source (Windows NPS on AD)  | DM_030                |
| `cisco-ise`           | Enterprise RADIUS replacement (NAC)   | DM_030                |
| `aruba-clearpass`     | Enterprise RADIUS replacement         | DM_030                |
| `entra-radius`        | Cloud-native RADIUS proxy             | DM_030                |

## Related controls

| Control     | Topic                                        |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------|
| **AC-18**   | Wireless Access (WPA3-Enterprise / 802.1X)   |
| **IA-3**    | Device Identification and Authentication     |
| **IA-5(2)** | Public Key-Based Authentication (cert-issuance for 802.1X) |
| **SC-7(5)** | Boundary Protection — Deny by Default        |

## What goes here when this scaffold is filled in

A complete G.6 guide will mirror the structure of
[G.3 DNS Modernization](dns-modernization.qmd):

1. **Landscape decomposition** — current NPS policies, RADIUS clients
   (APs, VPN concentrators, switches), AD-group dependencies, EAP-method
   inventory.
2. **Target architecture** — which workloads land on Cisco ISE, which
   land on Entra RADIUS Proxy, which retire to Conditional Access /
   ZTNA.
3. **Migration sequence** — parallel-run validation, cutover order,
   30-day decommission window for NPS.
4. **Validation evidence** — RADIUS-trust update for SCEP-issued device
   certificates (see IA-5(2)), 802.1X auth-success rates by EAP method,
   AAA-source attestation.
5. **Rollback** — conditions for restoring NPS, and how to do so
   without re-introducing the AD dependency.

## See also

- [DM_030 RADIUS / NPS Adapter Interface](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/adapters/radius/radius-adapter-interface.md)
  — canonical adapter spec
- [Directory Migration Adapters — RADIUS](../../../modernization/adapters.qmd#radius)
  — narrative view with priority matrix
- [G. Network Transformation — index](index.qmd) — sibling leaves
